name: Spacing
description: Sites are using predefined vertical spacing tokens ensuring content is properly positioned on the baseline grid. All levels of architectural elementsv have a set of predefined spacing tokens allowing to maintain vertical consistency.
content:
## **Composition model**

  

**Padding values:**

Vertical spacing on a page follows consistent rules and has predefined padding values:

*   deep (128 px)
*   regular (64 px)
*   shallow (24 px)
*   none (0 px)

  

**Vertical spacing:**

The padding is applied to content layout as follows:

1.  **Hero top padding (shallow - 24 px):** to ensure proper spacing between first section and top navigation shallow padding is used for the first element appearing on the page.
2.  **Spacing between sections regulated by the bottom padding (regular - 64 px):** each section is followed by 64 pixels high space separating visually sections from each other. This does not apply to the last section of the page (5).
3.  **Top padding (none - 0 px) :** regular sections do not use any spacing as a top padding. Hero section (1) and CTA sections (using deep top and bottom padding) are the only exceptions.  
4.  **Blocks and UI components padding:** UI elements and blocks use baked in padding to ensure vertical spacing control between them.

**Last section bottom padding (deep - 128 px):** last section on the page is followed by bigger spacing to ensure proper visual balance of the content layout distribution on the page.

![Spacing.png](images/a2e1e58d-e82a-4444-8ec9-dd2cac9d36c1.png)

appliedRules.rule: Vertical spacing is regulated exclusively by the bottom padding.
Bottom padding of a section is 64 px (regular) - except the last section for which it is 128 px (deep).
Layouts must preserve consistent vertical spacing between sections. Do not manually reduce spacing to fit more content "above the fold".
Sites’ system uses predefined spacing applied to the page as a whole as well as to particular sections.
Section padding has 4 possible values: deep (128px) / regular (64px) / shallow (24px) or none (0px).
